prompt: isometric pixel-art game tiles for a top-down RPG map. Each tile is a DIFFERENT medium-sized object standing FREE on a small flat patch of packed brown dirt — the object itself rises straight up and IS the sprite. Do NOT put it on a raised block, cube, column, box, crate or pedestal, and do NOT draw the ground as a tall raised cube: just a small low patch at the base, everything else transparent. Go for maximum variety — different shapes, species, ages and conditions, no two tiles alike. The set: 1) a rusted plough half-sunk in dirt 2) a wooden well with a bucket 3) a dry standing dead snag 4) a dry twisted shrub 5) a leaning fence post 6) a tall barrel cactus 7) a stack of clay bricks 8) a bare dead tree 9) a dead tree with a bird's nest 10) a dirt mound topped with a skull. cozy hand-crafted top-down RPG pixel art, rich natural texture, cohesive harmonious palette, soft directional shading, high quality game asset, transparent background
